creator: dorian.buettner@gmx.de
text:
Some good points, indeed. Whatever, cups-base was the first port I saw 
that problem, and I can't find a hint that port upgrading tools 
(portmaster, ...) are exclusive to nscd.
So, the patch was just a proposal to handle this. If other ports are hit 
by this too, then propably a global solution would be the way to go. 
Which one would be the place to have this discussed? Ports-mailing list?

Incoherence of course is somewhat a deal taken into account for 
performance gain. I get the impression, when going through several man 
pages, that propably nsswitch should take of cleaning the cache when 
manipulating commands arrive, that is add, delete,modify?
The pw manpage states that pw is to operate on local files only, but it 
doesn't, it took the query result out of the cache.
